heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen today, marking an exceptional precipitation event and it ranked top out of our records.  A rate of 24.6mm per hour was recorded during the 144.8 hour long storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 24.6mm per hour was the peak rain rate recorded today ranking 2nd in our overall records.  The storm lasted 144.8 hours and 47.89mm of rain fell.  On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: Today's rainstorm ranked first on our record table for the length of the storm as it lasted 144.8 hours; it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 23, which occurred on 07 September 2025 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: Stormy conditions are expected.  This is the first storm in the region since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest rainy periods recorded.  This rainy period is ranked 8th since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 07 November 2025 and has lasted 9 days.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 6 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (182°), which differs from the daily average south-westerly (229.0°) by approximately 47.0 degrees.    In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.5°C) and outside temperature (down 1.5°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (up 0.1 mm) and cloud levels (up 268 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 137.6m higher than yesterday, 18.8m lower than the past week, 267.6m lower than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 561m and a peak of 715m, averaging 749.2m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; mild temperatures are expected. Also, pleasant humidity levels and mostly calm with light air movement. Chance of light rain, fog or low cloud is expected, and periods of sunshine are expected. Weather could become unsettled.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (95%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
